title: fagus grandifolia var. caroliniana
intro: Fagus grandifolia var. caroliniana, also known as the American beech tree, is a deciduous tree native to the southeastern United States. The bark and leaves of this plant have been used in traditional medicine, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it effective in treating various health issues. The health benefits of Fagus grandifolia var. caroliniana include reducing fever, alleviating respiratory problems, and supporting the immune system. Herbal preparations of this plant are typically made by infusing the bark and leaves in hot water to create a tea, or by using the extracts in tinctures and capsules.
